summary: African-American playwright, author of the ten-play Pittsburgh Cycle (Century Cycle) depicting Black life in each decade of the 20th century. Most commonly identified by Fences (Troy Maxson, garbage collector, Rose, Cory, Gabriel's trumpet) and The Piano Lesson (Boy Willie, Berniece, Ghosts of the Yellow Dog, Sutter's ghost). Other plays include Ma Rainey's Black Bottom, Joe Turner's Come and Gone, Two Trains Running, Jitney, Seven Guitars, King Hedley II, Gem of the Ocean, and Radio Golf.

works.name: Fences
works.description: Wilson's most famous play, set in 1950s Pittsburgh. Troy Maxson is a garbage collector and former baseball player who couldn't break into the MLB due to the color barrier. He prevents his son Cory from playing college football, forcing him to work at A&P. Troy has an affair with Alberta, who dies in childbirth; Rose adopts their daughter Raynell. Troy's brother Gabriel has a metal plate in his head from WWII and believes he's an archangel. The play ends at Troy's funeral, where Gabriel blows his broken trumpet and the 'gates of Heaven' open as he dances. Troy tells Rose he 'planted a seed' but 'the soil was hard and rocky.' Troy refers to death as 'nothing but a fastball on the outside corner.' Bono buys a fridge after Troy finishes building the fence.

clues.clue: Troy Maxson: garbage collector, former baseball player; color barrier prevented MLB; son Cory forbidden from playing football

works.name: The Piano Lesson
works.description: Boy Willie wants to sell the family piano (which has the family's history carved into it by their slave ancestors) to buy Sutter's land. His sister Berniece refuses. Sutter's ghost appears and is exorcised when Berniece plays the piano. The Ghosts of the Yellow Dog (Boy Charles and four hobos burned alive in a boxcar by a lynch mob) are blamed for mysterious deaths including Sutter falling down a well. Wining Boy sells clothes to Lymon. The play features a 'magic suit.'

clues.clue: Boy Willie vs. Berniece over selling the piano; family history carved into it by slave ancestors

works.name: Ma Rainey's Black Bottom
works.description: The only Pittsburgh Cycle play not set in Pittsburgh (set in Chicago). Blues musicians wait for Ma Rainey to start a recording session; she refuses until she gets a Coca-Cola. The trumpeter Levee stabs Toledo for stepping on his Florsheim shoes. Stuttering Sylvester appears. Slow Drag. Adapted into a 2020 film starring Viola Davis and Chadwick Boseman.

clues.clue: Only Pittsburgh Cycle play not set in Pittsburgh; set in Chicago; Ma Rainey refuses to start recording without Coca-Cola

works.name: Joe Turner's Come and Gone
works.description: Set in the 1910s at Seth Holly's boarding house. Herald Loomis searches for his wife Martha Pentecost after being captured and enslaved by Joe Turner for seven years. The conjure man Bynum sings a song about Joe Turner. The 'People Finder' Selig is a sheet metal seller. Herald slashes his chest and rubs blood on his face, finding his 'song of self-sufficiency.' Bynum describes following a 'shiny man.' Reuben and Zonia look at pigeons. Working title was Mill Hand's Lunch Bucket, inspired by Romare Bearden painting.

works.name: Other Plays and General
works.description: Two Trains Running (Memphis Lee's diner; Risa scars her legs; Sterling steals ham for Hambone; Hambone can only say two phrases about ham). Jitney (unlicensed taxi business; Becker; Booster; Youngblood buys house for Rena). Seven Guitars (Floyd 'Schoolboy' Barton; King Hedley kills Floyd mistaking him for Buddy Bolden). King Hedley II (sells stolen refrigerators; Stool Pigeon; Aunt Ester dies at 366). Gem of the Ocean (Aunt Ester at 1839 Wylie Avenue; Citizen Barlow; City of Bones). Radio Golf (Harmond Wilks campaigns for mayor; 1839 Wylie illegally acquired). Aunt Ester is a recurring clairvoyant character who ages from 285 to 366 across the cycle. Wilson's 'four B's' influences include Romare Bearden. Wilson slammed Robert Brustein in 'The Ground on Which I Stand.' Plays often feature 'spectacle characters.'

clues.clue: Aunt Ester: recurring clairvoyant; ages from 285 to 366; lives at 1839 Wylie Avenue

comprehensive_summary:
August Wilson is the most frequently clued African-American playwright in quizbowl, known for his ten-play Pittsburgh Cycle depicting Black life in each decade of the 20th century. Fences is by far the most heavily clued play: Troy Maxson is a garbage collector and former baseball player who couldn't enter the MLB due to the color barrier, prevents his son Cory from playing football, and has an affair with Alberta. Troy's brother Gabriel, brain-damaged from WWII, blows his broken trumpet at Troy's funeral and the 'gates of Heaven' open.

The Piano Lesson is the second most clued play, centering on the conflict between Boy Willie (who wants to sell the family piano to buy land) and Berniece (who refuses). The Ghosts of the Yellow Dog — Boy Charles and four hobos burned alive in a boxcar by a lynch mob — are blamed for mysterious deaths. Berniece ultimately plays the piano to exorcise Sutter's ghost.

Joe Turner's Come and Gone features Herald Loomis searching for his wife after seven years of enslavement by Joe Turner; he slashes his chest finding his 'song of self-sufficiency.' Ma Rainey's Black Bottom, uniquely set in Chicago rather than Pittsburgh, features the trumpeter Levee stabbing Toledo over Florsheim shoes. Two Trains Running features Hambone and Sterling. Seven Guitars and King Hedley II explore generational violence. Gem of the Ocean features Aunt Ester, the recurring clairvoyant who lives at 1839 Wylie Avenue (address inspired by the Amistad Revolt).

Play: brain-damaged WWII veteran with metal plate in head blows a broken trumpet to open the gates of Heaven at his brother's funeral
Play: Boy Willie tries to sell a piano with family history carved into it by slave ancestors; sister Berniece plays it to exorcise Sutter's ghost
Play: Boy Charles and four hobos burned alive in a boxcar become the Ghosts of the Yellow Dog, blamed for mysterious deaths
Play: Herald Loomis searches for wife Martha Pentecost after seven years of enslavement; slashes his chest finding his 'song of self-sufficiency'
cards.back: Joe Turner's Come and Gone (August Wilson)
